verdict_reason: BG 2.68's tags are focus-practice, deep-work, operator-system-coupling. CROSS-REFERENCE WITH SAME-TAG VERSES: (a) operator-system-coupling is shared with 2.48 ('the operator-state did not move' across green/red dashboards). 2.68 teaches: prajñā stabilises when the attention surfaces are architected so that input does not couple uncontrolled to operator-state. 2.48 teaches: the operator-state should not move with system-state during incidents. These are COMPATIBLE — both name the structural decoupling of operator-state from input-state, with 2.68 specifying the architectural mechanism. (b) focus-practice and deep-work are shared with 2.67 (immediately preceding negative case). 2.67 names the loss-mechanism; 2.68 names the positive corollary. Coherent verse-pair under the doctrine of input-stream architecture. CROSS-REFERENCES: 2.68 references 2.55, 2.57, 2.58, 2.61 via the pratiṣṭhitā cross-verse pattern claim — all are within the sthita-prajña block; the claim is structurally correct (the source pack's traditional_meaning_consensus confirms). Pass on both 6.1 and 6.5.
